title: The Cyclic (2869,240,20) Difference-Set Case
lean_gate_reason: Lean would formalize the verified mod-151 supporting slice, but it would not resolve the selected existence problem or lift the packet to HUMAN_READY. Formalization is optional archival polish until a coupled 19x151 obstruction or exact lift argument closes the row.
micro_paper_assessment: Not paper-ready. The verified mod-151 slice is structural and exact at the slice level, but it is not most of a publishable micro-paper because the exact cyclic (2869,240,20) existence question remains open. Preserve it as support and move the slug aside unless a concrete coupled obstruction is already in hand.
family_affinity: cyclic prime-product residual cases with multiplier-forced prime-quotient contractions of Paley type
strongest_honest_claim: If a cyclic (2869,240,20) difference set exists, then after multiplier normalization its contraction modulo 151 is uniquely forced, up to swapping quadratic residues and nonresidues, to satisfy c_0 = 15, c_x = 2 on one square class, and c_x = 1 on the other.
paper_title_hint: A forced mod-151 Paley-type quotient profile in the cyclic (2869,240,20) difference-set problem
candidate_theorem_slice: Any cyclic (2869,240,20) difference set has, modulo 151, a unique multiplier-normalized contraction up to swapping quadratic residues and nonresidues: c_0 = 15, c_x = 2 on one square class, and c_x = 1 on the other.
next_action: preserve_variant_move_aside; reopen only for a concrete coupled 19x151 obstruction or exact lift contradiction; do not expand into a broader theorem program
